    "internal_reasoning": "This painting presents one of the most recognizable stylistic signatures in 20th-century art history. The work depicts a reclining female nude rendered in a highly distinctive manner that immediately calls to mind the work of Amedeo Modigliani (1884–1920), the Italian-born School of Paris painter. Let me analyze the visual evidence systematically.

STYLISTIC ANALYSIS:
The figure displays the hallmark elongation and sinuous contour lines associated with Modigliani's mature nude period (approximately 1916–1919). The face shows the characteristic almond-shaped eyes — here rendered with irises and a direct gaze, which Modigliani sometimes included — the long nose, small pursed lips, and swan-like neck. The warm, ochre-toned flesh rendering with subtle warm-to-cool transitions is consistent with his palette. The background employs deep crimson/burgundy tones against which the figure is dramatically set, another hallmark of his reclining nude compositions.

The pose — arms raised behind the head, body turned slightly toward the viewer, reclining on cushions — is nearly identical to several well-documented Modigliani nudes from his most celebrated series, including works now housed in major museum collections worldwide. The brushwork shows confident, fluid application with relatively smooth modeling of the skin punctuated by darker contour lines.

COMPARABLES AND MARKET CONTEXT:
Modigliani's reclining nudes represent some of the most valuable works in the entire art market. Key auction comparables include:
- 'Nu couché' (1917-18) sold at Christie's New York in November 2015 for $170.4 million — a world record at the time
- 'Nu couché (sur le côté gauche)' sold at Sotheby's New York in May 2018 for $157.2 million
- Various other reclining nudes have sold in the $40–80 million range at major houses

The specific work visible here appears consistent with Modigliani's output from approximately 1917–1919. Based on compositional similarity, scale, and quality of execution visible, this appears to be a significant example from his nude series.

CONDITION OBSERVATIONS:
From the image, the paint surface appears relatively stable with good overall preservation. The colors retain vibrancy. No obvious major losses or restoration areas are visible, though a proper condition report would require in-person examination under raking light and UV.

PROVENANCE CONSIDERATIONS:
Modigliani nudes carry significant provenance scrutiny requirements. Any work coming to market would require thorough due diligence regarding WWII-era ownership history and legal title.

VALUATION:
Given the extraordinary market performance of Modigliani reclining nudes, the quality of execution visible, and current market conditions (which have seen some softening since the 2015-2018 peaks), I estimate a fair market value in the range of $80–150 million for a fully authenticated, clean-provenance example of this quality. I'll place my point estimate at approximately $120 million, acknowledging significant uncertainty depending on exact provenance, exhibition history, and precise dating within the series.",

parsed.internal_reasoning:
This painting presents one of the most recognizable stylistic signatures in 20th-century art history. The work depicts a reclining female nude rendered in a highly distinctive manner that immediately calls to mind the work of Amedeo Modigliani (1884–1920), the Italian-born School of Paris painter. Let me analyze the visual evidence systematically.

STYLISTIC ANALYSIS:
The figure displays the hallmark elongation and sinuous contour lines associated with Modigliani's mature nude period (approximately 1916–1919). The face shows the characteristic almond-shaped eyes — here rendered with irises and a direct gaze, which Modigliani sometimes included — the long nose, small pursed lips, and swan-like neck. The warm, ochre-toned flesh rendering with subtle warm-to-cool transitions is consistent with his palette. The background employs deep crimson/burgundy tones against which the figure is dramatically set, another hallmark of his reclining nude compositions.

The pose — arms raised behind the head, body turned slightly toward the viewer, reclining on cushions — is nearly identical to several well-documented Modigliani nudes from his most celebrated series, including works now housed in major museum collections worldwide. The brushwork shows confident, fluid application with relatively smooth modeling of the skin punctuated by darker contour lines.

COMPARABLES AND MARKET CONTEXT:
Modigliani's reclining nudes represent some of the most valuable works in the entire art market. Key auction comparables include:
- 'Nu couché' (1917-18) sold at Christie's New York in November 2015 for $170.4 million — a world record at the time
- 'Nu couché (sur le côté gauche)' sold at Sotheby's New York in May 2018 for $157.2 million
- Various other reclining nudes have sold in the $40–80 million range at major houses

The specific work visible here appears consistent with Modigliani's output from approximately 1917–1919. Based on compositional similarity, scale, and quality of execution visible, this appears to be a significant example from his nude series.

CONDITION OBSERVATIONS:
From the image, the paint surface appears relatively stable with good overall preservation. The colors retain vibrancy. No obvious major losses or restoration areas are visible, though a proper condition report would require in-person examination under raking light and UV.

PROVENANCE CONSIDERATIONS:
Modigliani nudes carry significant provenance scrutiny requirements. Any work coming to market would require thorough due diligence regarding WWII-era ownership history and legal title.

VALUATION:
Given the extraordinary market performance of Modigliani reclining nudes, the quality of execution visible, and current market conditions (which have seen some softening since the 2015-2018 peaks), I estimate a fair market value in the range of $80–150 million for a fully authenticated, clean-provenance example of this quality. I'll place my point estimate at approximately $120 million, acknowledging significant uncertainty depending on exact provenance, exhibition history, and precise dating within the series.
key_factors: Characteristic Modigliani stylistic elements: elongated forms, sinuous contours, warm ochre flesh tones, crimson background — consistent with his 1917-1919 nude series
Reclining female nudes by Modigliani represent some of the highest-value works in the global art market, with recent auction records between $80M and $170M
Condition appears good from visual inspection — stable paint surface, vibrant color retention, no obvious major losses
Authentication and provenance research would be critical determinants of final value — Modigliani works face significant scrutiny
Current market conditions show some softening from 2015-2018 peak prices, tempering the upper end of comparable valuations
